name: Girls Rebel
description: A film program celebrating rebellious female characters and groundbreaking women filmmakers throughout history.

name: Astronomy 102: Stars, Galaxies, Cosmos
description: This introductory course explores objects beyond the solar system, including the properties of light and gravitation. Students will survey celestial bodies like stars and galaxies to learn how astronomers gather cosmic information.

name: Member Walk on the Wild Side
description: A brisk morning walk through the Museum's halls followed by a light exercise class before the building opens to the public.
short_name: Member Walk on the Wild Side

name: Woodshop Safety Check
description: Mandatory hands-on orientation covering the essentials of using the woodshop safely and effectively. Completion of general orientation is required first.

name: Environmental Protection Committee
description: A monthly meeting to discuss environmental protection, sanitation, and sustainability issues within the district.

name: Health and Social Services Committee
description: Meeting focused on local health services, social programs, and community welfare initiatives.

name: Public Safety & Sanitation Committee Meeting
description: Considers proposed state legislation regarding court-mandated treatment for mental illness and general public safety issues.

name: Land Use & Zoning Committee Meeting
description: This committee meets to review zoning changes, land use applications, and urban development projects in the district.

name: NYC Must-See Week
description: Rockefeller Center offers 2-for-1 deals on Top of the Rock tickets, ice skating sessions, and guided tours as part of a citywide celebration of cultural attractions.

name: intenSati
description: A heart-pumping cardio class that blends dance, kickboxing, and yoga with positive affirmations.

name: JUXTAPOSE | A Theatrical Shadow Box
description: A visually stunning performance using shadow box techniques to create theatrical magic by Happenstance Theater.

name: Downtown Beats Chorus
description: This adult chorus is open to all who love to sing and is directed by the Church Street School of Music. Members learn contemporary and classic songs for community performances.

name: Queer Personal Stories
description: An intimate session where community members share their personal journeys and narratives in a supportive environment.

name: Family Story Time
description: Join a dynamic story time session for kids ages two and up featuring artful tales and movement activities.

name: A Time for Everything: Guided Exhibition Tour
description: Join a guided tour of Scandinavia House's 25th Anniversary exhibition to learn about the participating artists and their featured works.

name: Magnet Community Mixer
description: This improv mixer is open to everyone, where performers can sign up to jump on stage regardless of experience level.

name: Megawatt: Rumi & Neptune
description: Hand-picked ensembles perform high-powered long-form improv including Harolds, monoscenes, and everything in between.

name: Wacky Wednesdays
description: Happy Hour All Night.

name: Mommy and Me Classes
description: Mommy and Me Classes for children up to 4 years old.

name: DUH!
description: Relatable sketch comedy that pushes boundaries and explores the absurdities of daily life.

name: Ches Smith Quartet
description: Percussionist Ches Smith leads a quartet with Mary Halvorson, Liberty Ellman, and Nick Dunston. The group performs different music every night during this residency.

name: David Ostwald’s Louis Armstrong Eternity Band
description: Celebrate the legacy of Satchmo with this ensemble dedicated to the traditional New Orleans jazz style popularized by Louis Armstrong.
short_name: David Ostwald’s Louis Armstrong Eternity Band

name: Committee 3: Health, Education, & Cultural Affairs
description: Committee meeting covering health and human services, education, youth services, and cultural affairs.

name: City Services Planning Work Group
description: A community board meeting focused on planning and reviewing city services for the local district.

name: Parks, Recreation and Culture Committee
description: Committee discussion regarding neighborhood parks, recreational facilities, and local cultural programming.

name: Public Safety Committee
description: Meeting with local agencies and residents to address neighborhood safety and quality-of-life concerns.

name: Health & Education Committee Meeting
description: Focuses on health insurance for retired city employees and Bellevue Hospital's budget priorities for the coming year.

name: Consumer Affairs Committee Meeting
description: A meeting to review business licenses, sidewalk cafe applications, and other consumer-related regulations affecting the local area.

name: Try/Step/Trip
description: A concept musical performed through the language of step that reimagines a young man's experience in a court-ordered drug rehabilitation program.

name: Mah Jongg Lessons
description: Learn the rules and mechanics of American Mah Jongg in this two-hour group lesson. Registration is required for this strategy-based tile game session led by Susan Richards.

name: Fiber Art Crafts Studio
description: Bring your knitting, crocheting, or embroidery projects for an afternoon of creativity and camaraderie. These sessions offer seniors a chance to learn new skills and socialize.

name: Botany for Horticulturists
description: An introductory course that requires no previous knowledge of botany, serving as a foundation for the Certificate in Horticulture program.

name: Music Producer/ Song Writer Circle
description: A collaborative space for LGBTQ+ music producers and songwriters to share their work and receive feedback.

name: Private Writings, Public Readings Book Club
description: The group will discuss Iris Origo's "A Chill in the Air: An Italian War Diary, 1939-1940." The club explores the nature of diaries and journals as intended public or private works.
short_name: Private Writings, Public Readings Book Club

name: Pasta Making & Cheese Tasting
description: Master the art of pasta rolling while enjoying a guided tasting of artisan cheeses. This class combines hands-on culinary skills with Murray's world-class cheese expertise.

name: Open Art Space
description: A weekly drop-in program for LGBTQ+ high school students and allies to make art in a welcoming environment.

name: Vixen! (1968)
description: Russ Meyer's 1968 film followed by a conversation with Erica Gavin and Peggy Ahwesh.

name: Pastel Workshop with Margarethe Aanestad
description: Explore and experiment with various pastel chalks on paper in this hands-on workshop led by artist Margarethe Aanestad.

name: Only A God Can Save Us
description: This exhibition features multi-media works by Generation Z artists who critically engage with reappropriated imagery and contemporary tonality.
short_name: Only A God Can Save Us

name: Sound of Color: Art + Music Afterschool Program
description: This program for preschoolers and their families explores how music and art intertwine through gallery tours and studio art-making. It is led by both museum and music educators.

name: Teen Art Hang
description: An open and creative space for NYC teens to work on thematic art projects or independent work in a studio setting. Snacks and materials are provided for this free drop-in session.

name: Musical Megawatt: Houndstooth & Grinch Debussy
description: A night entirely dedicated to musical improv, featuring rotations of new, never-before-seen musicals made up on the spot.

name: Thirsty Thursdays
description: Happy Hour All Night.

name: Pit Ensembles
description: Experience live long-form and musical improvisation featuring the PIT's talented House Teams.

name: Bob and Larry's
description: A "where are they now" look at favorite childhood TV characters who are now struggling adults.

name: Bank of America Winter Village Ice Skating
description: Skate at Manhattan's winter wonderland on New York City's only free-admission ice rink. The Rink is open daily through March 1 and requires advanced online reservations for all skating sessions.
short_name: Bank of America Winter Village Ice Skating

name: Bryant Park Shop
description: Bring home the park year-round with lively t-shirts, socks, home goods, and ornaments. All proceeds from the Bryant Park Shop go back into supporting the park's free community programs.

name: Open Hours
description: Visit the studio during open hours to play mahjong and enjoy the community space.

name: Marjorie Prime
description: Pulitzer Prize finalist Jordan Harrison reinvents the family drama in this powerful, wryly funny rumination on aging, artificial intelligence, and memory. Directed by Tony Award nominee Anne Kauffman, the play explores the blurred line between a life lived and a life remembered.

name: TUMI Semi Annual Sale
description: Enjoy an extra 40% off select styles during TUMI's Semi-Annual Sale for a limited time. Additional markdowns have been added to their collection of premium travel and lifestyle accessories.

name: Tour the Research Vessel Robert Gray
description: Step onto the deck of a working research vessel for guided dockside tours. Discover the ship's history, explore its three decks, and learn about its role in groundbreaking marine research and exploration.
short_name: Tour the Research Vessel Robert Gray

name: The New York Sari
description: An exploration of the history and cultural significance of the sari in New York, featuring photographs of community life.

name: The Gay Harlem Renaissance
description: A look at the LGBTQ+ history and performers of the Harlem Renaissance, featuring figures like drag performer Gladys Bentley.

name: Declaring the Revolution: America’s Printed Path to Independence
description: This exhibition examines the printed documents and paths that led to American independence.

name: Holiday Express: Toys and Trains from the Jerni Collection
description: The annual holiday exhibition featuring a massive display of vintage toys and model trains from the Jerni Collection.
short_name: Toys and Trains from the Jerni Collection

name: Stirring the Melting Pot: Photographs from The New York Historical Collections
description: A photographic look at the evolving urban landscape and immigration history of New York through the museum's archives.
short_name: Photographs from The New York Historical Collections

name: The Recordings: Voices from the Shoah Tapes
description: An audio-focused exhibition featuring recordings and voices from the Shoah archives.

name: Committee Meeting: Economic Development Technologies and Communications
description: This committee focuses on neighborhood economic growth, technology integration, and communications strategies. Meetings require a mandatory identification for entry and seating is available on a first-come, first-served basis.

name: CB2 Board Meeting
description: The full board of Queens Community Board 2 meets monthly to discuss district-wide concerns, review committee reports, and vote on community matters.

name: ONEG: A Shabbat Experience at 92NY
description: A Friday night program featuring Kabbalat Shabbat blessings, live music, community dialogue, and a kosher-style meal.

name: Midtown Art Deco Tour: The Golden Age of the Skyscraper
description: Discover the golden age of skyscrapers by visiting stunning Art Deco landmarks and lobbies in Midtown. Learn how the architecture of the 20th century evolved and defined the iconic skyline.
short_name: The Golden Age of the Skyscraper

name: "Great British Bake Off" Cake Week
description: Bake and enjoy tea while mastering the cake techniques featured in the Great British Bake Off.

name: How to Cook Pasta Like a Chef
description: A social cooking experience designed to help you meet others while learning the art of pasta making.
short_name: How to Cook Pasta Like a Chef

name: Natalie Tenenbaum: Artist in Residence
description: Composer and pianist Natalie Tenenbaum begins a five-week residency traveling fluidly between classical, jazz, pop, and experimental soundscapes. Her boundary-crossing approach reimagines familiar music in a wholly new way.

name: Ej the DJ
description: Forest Döwn Under presents a dynamic Friday night featuring EJ The DJ and Little Fax The Machine. Expect a night of cutting-edge electronic sounds and club hits.

name: Asian Climbing Tribe
description: A safe space for the Asian community to connect, network, and address community issues through climbing.

name: MAD for a Cause: Project Street Beat
description: A community-focused event at the museum supporting the Project Street Beat initiative.
short_name: MAD for a Cause: Project Street Beat

name: Improv Level One Intensive Class Show
description: Graduates of the beginner improv intensive showcase the foundational skills they have learned.
short_name: Improv Level One Intensive Class Show

name: Pink Pier at Watermark
description: Visit this fully immersive pink-themed waterfront destination featuring festive cocktails and photo-ready decor.

name: Washington Heights - Sugar Hill North Harlem Walking Tour
description: A Friday morning exploration of northern Harlem, focusing on the cultural and historical landscape of Washington Heights and North Sugar Hill.
short_name: Washington Heights - Sugar Hill North Harlem Walking Tour

name: Forest Fridays @ Van Cortlandt Park
description: Join volunteers to save the forest floor by removing invasive species like Bush Honeysuckle and Multiflora Rose. This winter initiative is critical for protecting native plants while they are dormant.

name: Volunteering at Conference House Park - The Blue Wave
description: Participate in a self-directed beach and park clean-up initiative. Volunteers help remove debris and maintain the beauty of the Staten Island waterfront.

name: Ceramics Studio Time - 4-session access block
description: Flexible open studio access block for ceramic artists to work on independent projects with access to pottery wheels and hand-building tables. Clay must be purchased through the makerspace and glaze firing fees apply based on size.

name: Shelter from the Storm: An Artist’s Eye with Stan Allen and Polly Apfelbaum
description: Shelter from the Storm features a collaborative effort between Stan Allen and Polly Apfelbaum, blending architecture and contemporary art. The show examines thematic links between environment, protection, and spatial aesthetics.

name: Revisiting the World of Tomorrow: The 1964-65 World's Fair
location: NYC Department of Records and Information Services (DORIS)
description: An onsite exhibit exploring the 1964-1965 World’s Fair through images, brochures, and ephemera from the Municipal Archives. The display focuses on key areas of the fair including transportation, religion, and commerce.

name: Windows on the Archives
location: NYC Department of Records and Information Services (DORIS)
description: A permanent onsite exhibit displaying the breadth of the City's documentary treasures from the Municipal Archives. The exhibit features records spanning over 300 years of governing and protecting the people of New York City.

name: WSCAH’s 86th Street Market
location: St. Paul & St. Andrew United Methodist Church
description: Each month, customers can receive a regular distribution of USDA MyPlate Balanced groceries. These packages contain a variety of healthy items, including grains, proteins, dairy products, and fresh fruits and vegetables.

name: Primeval Ground [deep, unfathomable]
description: A group exhibition featuring ten artists exploring monochrome and low-chrome artworks across media like photography, weaving, and sculpture. The exhibit includes a panel discussion on Jan 15 and a movement drawing workshop on Jan 24.

name: Video of “Stories Around the Table”
description: A filmed performance featuring current and past members of a writing group that has met for 40 years to discuss aging and creativity. The collective share personal stories sitting around the table, reflecting a rich history of theatrical and literary collaboration.

name: Calling Choreographers: WestFest Dance 2026 Open Call
description: An invitation for choreographers to submit applications for the WestFest Dance 2026 festival. This online application period is open to performing artists seeking to showcase their work in the upcoming season.

name: Somewhere Funny, Nowhere Serious!
description: Somewhere Nowhere NYC transforms into an intimate speakeasy-style comedy club featuring a live stand-up showcase with big-name comedians as seen on Netflix and HBO.

name: Winter Realm Cinema
description: Winter Realm Cinema is a seasonal rooftop film experience in Midtown featuring a fully enclosed and heated terrace with stunning Manhattan views. The program offers a curated selection of cult films, classics, and new releases enjoyed via state-of-the-art wireless headphones. Guests can also enjoy a full bar and classic cinema concessions like fresh popcorn and hot dogs through March 1st.

name: Eternal Nature: Immersive Digital Art Exhibition
description: Experience 'Eternal Nature,' a multisensory digital art exhibition featuring immersive landscapes like crashing waterfalls and blooming forests. The gallery also showcases a landmark collaboration with the Musée d'Orsay, reimagining masterpieces by Monet and Van Gogh through light, sound, and scent.

name: Penguin Feeding
description: Join zoo staff to learn about the diet and habits of the zoo's resident penguins during their scheduled afternoon meal.

name: Junior Tennis Development Program
description: A comprehensive after-school tennis program for students aged 7 to 17, focusing on technical drills, strategic points, and match play. Participants are placed based on age and ability to ensure individual attention and development of sportsmanship.

name: Karaoke Friday
description: Join the Gottscheer Hall community for their signature Friday night karaoke. Experience a wide selection of German beers and pub eats while enjoying or participating in an evening of singing and entertainment.

name: Live Music
description: Grand Vin hosts live music performances Wednesday through Saturday nights, creating a sophisticated ambiance for dining and drinks. Guests can enjoy a rotating selection of musicians while exploring the venue's curated wine list and Italian-inspired menu.

name: Nutcracker Rouge
description: A perennial smash hit burlesque reimagining of the classic holiday tale featuring a hedonistic mix of circus, opera, and cabaret. This Drama Desk-nominated production is a sensual blend of music hall and theater in an opulent Brooklyn palace.

name: Cocktail Magique
description: A spectacular farewell run of this playful burlesque revue that blends world-class magic with tantalizing variety acts and bespoke cocktails. After over 575 performances, this immersive production takes its final bow on February 8.

name: The Housemaid
description: Millie accepts a live-in housemaid job for a wealthy couple, but the dream job unravels into a dangerous game of secrets.

name: Rewind by Whisbe
description: A contemporary art installation by artist WhIsBe featuring oversized childhood toy sculptures ranging from 6 to 16 feet tall. The display includes pieces like a 10-foot rocket ship and a 15-foot dinosaur, reflecting on innocence and resilience.

name: Friday Feel-Good Improv Jam
description: Kick off the weekend with a high-energy, supportive improv jam open to all. Whether you're a seasoned pro or a first-timer, this jam is all about having fun and making good choices on stage.

name: Character Counts Open Mic
description: Hosted by Liz Coin, this open mic is dedicated specifically to developing characters. Performers get five-minute slots to workshop existing characters or debut brand new ones.

name: UNIQLO Friday Nights
description: The museum stays open late every Friday night with free admission for New York State residents. Guests can enjoy exhibitions, drop-in drawing, film screenings, and unique shopping at the Design Store.

name: Smalls Afternoon Jam Session Hosted by Andrew Kushnir
description: An open jazz jam session featuring a professional house trio and hosted by drummer Andrew Kushnir. Musicians are invited to join the stage for an afternoon of communal music-making.
short_name: Smalls Afternoon Jam Session Hosted by Andrew Kushnir

name: Art + You: Gallery Conversations
description: A monthly session for visitors aged 18-30 to reflect and exchange perspectives on select artworks through guided dialogue. Each session is led by a Whitney Educator and focuses on intimate, thoughtful reflections of pieces currently on view.

name: Free Friday Night Hip Hop Dance Class & Party
description: Join Ailey Extension instructor TweetBoogie for a free hip hop dance class followed by a high-energy dance party featuring sets by DJ Bizzy. All ages and skill levels are welcome to participate in this vibrant Friday evening event.
short_name: Free Friday Night Hip Hop Dance Class & Party

name: Adult Cooking Class — Tasty Thai
description: Master the flavors of Thailand by preparing Pad Thai, Pineapple Chicken Satay with nut-free Thai sauce, and fresh veggie summer rolls. This BYOB workshop is entirely hands-on and designed for all skill levels.

name: Alpine Luxe: The Ski Chalet Experience at Industry Kitchen
description: Experience an upscale ski chalet vibe at Industry Kitchen, featuring seasonal alpine-inspired decor and a specialized winter menu. This immersive dining installation brings mountain luxury to the NYC waterfront.

name: Athi-Patra Ruga: Lord, I gotta keep on (movin’)
description: This multidisciplinary exhibition centers on myths, their creation, and their relationship to power and liberation through performance, film, painting, and textile. Ruga creates allegorical figures that draw from Xhosa history and post-apartheid South Africa to explore race and sexuality.

name: David Wojnarowicz: Arthur Rimbaud in New York
description: A series of photo-performances documenting New York City's decay in the late 1970s through the persona of poet Arthur Rimbaud. The exhibition features gelatin silver prints that intuitively connect the state of anarchy in post-Commune Paris with the artist's own predicament as a street survivor.

name: Soft Spaces
description: An on-view exhibition that explores contemporary queer identity and space through diverse artistic perspectives. The show engages with themes of the body and domesticity within the context of the museum's collection.

name: Artist Studios: Eva Tellier
description: Visit artist Eva Tellier in her studio to explore her latest works and creative process. This recurring studio residency allows visitors to engage directly with contemporary makers.

name: SUE: The T. rex Experience
description: Step into the world of SUE, the most complete and best-preserved Tyrannosaurus rex ever discovered, featuring a 40-foot-long skeleton replica. This interactive exhibition uses high-tech simulations and sensory effects to depict the prehistoric Cretaceous forest.

name: Fulton Fish Market Public Hours
description: Visit the historic Fulton Fish Market, one of the world's largest seafood wholesalers, during its early morning public hours. Explore a massive selection of fresh seafood and connect with specialized vendors in a unique wholesale environment. Visitors are encouraged to arrive before 5:00 AM for the best variety and supply availability.

name: Friday Night at MFAW
location: Music for a While Listening Bar & Lounge
description: Experience an immersive late-night music escape featuring stories and global sounds in an intimate underground setting. This Friday night session offers a unique listening bar atmosphere beneath the cosmopolitan heart of the city.

name: Joan Jonas: Moving off the Land II (Excerpts)
location: Columbia University Lenfest Center for the Arts
description: This multimedia exhibition features drawings of red fish by alumna Joan Jonas, scaled up into stunning banners. The installation explores the science and storytelling of the world's oceans as part of a yearlong 60th anniversary celebration.

name: A Night at the Museum: The Overnight Experience
description: Guests explore their favorite halls and fall asleep under the iconic Blue Whale or surrounded by other beloved Museum exhibits.

name: Seneca Village Educators' Tour
description: Join Central Park Conservancy guides for a tour exploring the history of the Seneca Village community, designed specifically for NYC educators. Participants will learn about the displacement of residents for the construction of the park.

name: Intro to MIG Welding: Half-Day Intensive - Brooklyn
description: Learn the fundamentals of Metal Inert Gas welding, the simplest form of joining steel. This hands-on workshop covers equipment setup, safety, and basic welding beads.

name: Machining 1: Learn to Mill with Phil (2 sessions)
description: A two-day intensive course teaching the basics of subtractive manufacturing using manual milling machines. Students will learn to qualify materials and create a pair of precision brass dice.

name: Joey Diaz: 62 & Still Slinging
description: Joey "Coco" Diaz brings his powerhouse stand-up comedy and legendary storytelling to the stage. Known for his wild and unpredictable performances, Diaz is a veteran of film, television, and hit podcasts.

name: Bookstore
description: A play exploring human connections and stories set within the quiet sanctuary of a bookstore environment.

name: Between Two Drummers I
description: This rhythm-focused showcase features dynamic percussion performances and intricate collaborative improvisation.

name: Meet Daniel Tiger
description: Meet Daniel Tiger for a special greeting and explore his neighborhood in this interactive exhibit for young learners.

name: Public Tour of "With a Single Step"
description: A guided gallery tour of MOCA’s core exhibition providing an overview of Chinese history in America from the 19th century to the present.
short_name: Public Tour of "With a Single Step"

name: Winter Knitting with Hilary Snell
description: Build on your knitting foundation this winter in biweekly classes designed to improve your technique and craft.

name: Studio Signs: From Now: A Collection in Context
description: A discussion related to the exhibition "Studio Signs: From Now: A Collection in Context."
short_name: From Now: A Collection in Context

name: Robinson's Readers: Story Time and Craft
description: Little kids (and their grown-ups), join us for a monthly educator-facilitated picture book story time and hands-on craft inspired by the life and legacy of Jackie Robinson.
short_name: Robinson's Readers: Story Time and Craft

name: Winter Birding Tour
description: Join expert birder Alexandra Wang for a guided tour to spot resident birds and seasonal winter visitors.

name: Knitting Foundation Class
description: Build on your knitting foundation this winter in classes led by Hilary Snell, held biweekly at Scandinavia House.

name: Lil' Studio
description: An early childhood program for kids under five featuring reading, singing, movement, and art making to build developmental skills.

name: USPML Learn-and-Play Meetup
description: The United States Professional Mahjong League hosts an inclusive session for players to learn and practice mahjong techniques.

name: Certain Restrictions Do Apply: New Textile Works
description: A landmark textile exhibition featuring new works by Carolyn Mazloomi and Sharon Kerry-Harlan. The exhibition mines the history of Black American pioneers and expands the narrative of quiltmaking and textile art.

name: Central Harlem Walking Tour
description: The definitive tour of the heart of Harlem, exploring landmarks and the vibrant cultural legacy of Central Harlem.

name: Guided Tour of The Jim Henson Exhibition
description: A special guided tour exploring the creative legacy of Jim Henson and his iconic work with the Muppets.
short_name: Guided Tour of The Jim Henson Exhibition

name: Muddy Paws Dog Adoption Event
description: Meet wonderful dogs available for adoption from Muddy Paws Rescue. Volunteer handlers will be on-site to answer questions about the animals and the adoption process.

name: Gallery Hours
description: BioBAT Art Space is open to the public every Saturday, offering visitors a chance to explore exhibitions at the intersection of biology, technology, ecology, and design. The gallery is located within the historic Brooklyn Army Terminal and features works from artists and design innovators.

name: Penn Station & Moynihan Train Hall Tour
description: Discover the architectural history of the original Penn Station and its modern counterpart, the Moynihan Train Hall. This tour traces remnants of the 1910 masterpiece and explores the rise of the landmarks preservation movement.
short_name: Penn Station & Moynihan Train Hall Tour

name: The Saturday Jam
description: The PIT's signature Saturday evening improv jam. It's an open-to-all session where improvisers can work on their skills and play in front of a live audience in a welcoming environment.

name: Ladies Stand-Up! (Men Sit Down) Open Mic
description: A weekly stand-up open mic dedicated specifically to female and non-binary comedians to hone their craft in a supportive, focused space.

name: Adult Cooking Class — Fresh Handmade Pasta
description: Learn the art of making fresh pasta from scratch, including fettuccine and spinach ricotta ravioli paired with a classic tomato sauce. Enjoy this social, vegetarian-friendly cooking experience with your favorite wine or beer.
short_name: Adult Cooking Class — Fresh Handmade Pasta

name: Coffee Bark
description: Coffee Bark is an opportunity for Brooklyn dog owners to gather in the park for treats and coffee. It provides a community space for networking and socializing among local pet owners.

name: Audubon Nature Exploration
description: Join the Alliance for nature education programs held on weekends for all ages. These discovery sessions help visitors learn about local wildlife and the park's ecosystem.

name: Artist Studios: Jayoung Yoon
description: Artist Jayoung Yoon opens her studio doors to the public every Saturday. Experience her unique approach to contemporary art and see her works in progress.

name: Lil' Studio
description: Parents and caregivers of children under five are invited to the Harlem Library for art-making and bonding activities. This program aims to bring museum-quality creative experiences directly into the local community.

name: Saturday Night at MFAW
location: Music for a While Listening Bar & Lounge
description: Join us for a curated evening of music and connection at this underground sanctuary. Saturday nights at MFAW transform the lounge into a vibrant tapestry of sounds, culture, and memories.

name: Gilded Age Mansions of Fifth Avenue
description: Explore the design and history of the grand urban palaces along Fifth Avenue and hear stories of the robber barons who built them.
short_name: Gilded Age Mansions of Fifth Avenue

name: Rashid Johnson’s Rotunda Stage Performance Series
description: Poets, spoken word performers, and vocalists activate Rashid Johnson’s Rotunda Stage in a series of weekly performances. This series brings dynamic literary and vocal art to the heart of the museum.
short_name: Rashid Johnson’s Rotunda Stage Performance Series

name: Maybe Happy Ending Tickets
description: Winner of 6 Tony Awards, including Best Musical, _Maybe Happy Ending_ is the story of a chance encounter that sparks connection, adventure, and maybe even love.

name: Astoria Philosophy Book Club
description: A community-driven discussion group exploring philosophical texts and big ideas in a casual, approachable setting.

name: Chelsea Market, High Line & Hudson Yards Food & History Tour
description: A guided walking tour that blends local food tastings with the rich architectural and social history of West Manhattan.
short_name: Chelsea Market, High Line & Hudson Yards Food & History Tour

name: Best of Brooklyn Stand-Up Comedy
description: Catch top-tier stand-up featuring pro and rising stars from HBO, Comedy Central, and Netflix in an intimate setting. Guests can enjoy social board games and a variety of drinks with no two-item minimum requirement.

name: New Talent/Weekly Stand Up Auditions
description: Support the future of comedy by attending a showcase featuring promising new talent and club auditions. This show features comedians newer to the club scene in front of a real audience with no bringer fee or extra strings attached.

name: Live Jazz at Nook
description: Relax and enjoy an evening of live jazz music performed by rotating artists. This weekly series offers a smooth and sophisticated atmosphere for music lovers.

name: Screenings from The Paley Archive
description: Experience curated daily screenings of iconic media from the Paley Center's extensive archive, showcasing television history and culture. These public presentations highlight rare broadcast treasures and influential programming from decades past.

name: Community Blood Drive
description: Celebrate National Blood Donor Month with MPA and community partners at this blood drive.

name: Volunteer Landscaping and Cleanup at Ridgewood Reservoir
description: Participate in community efforts to maintain the landscape and remove debris from the historic Ridgewood Reservoir.

name: Open Sew at The Castle
description: Join fellow sewing, quilting, and knitting enthusiasts for an afternoon of creativity and conversation. Bring your current project and enjoy a sunlit workspace with views of Little Neck Bay.

name: Second Sunday Castle Tour (1:00 PM)
description: Take a guided tour of one of NYC's finest Gothic Revival architectural treasures, originally built in 1887. Staff and volunteers lead this 45-minute exploration of the historic building.

name: Slow Play
description: Veteran improvisers take their time building a patient, detailed, and realistic world on stage.

name: Dig + Discover: Drop-in Program
description: Families can explore ancient artifacts in an immersive simulated archaeological dig site. Participants use trowels and brushes to uncover replicas of items from the museum's collection.

name: January Skills Series: French Cooking & Baking
description: Learn to master the art of French cooking and baking with free lessons hosted by Williams Sonoma. Each session focuses on a specific technique, covering everything from crepes and cassoulets to the art of the tart.

name: Dungeons n' Drafts One-Shot
description: Join a Dungeons & Dragons 5th Edition one-shot adventure where all experience levels are welcome and materials are provided. Enjoy a social gaming experience with local craft beverages at All Night Skate.

name: Sunday Worship
location: St. Paul & St. Andrew United Methodist Church
description: A weekly gathering in the sanctuary or online for a joyful, inspiring, thought-provoking, and intimate celebration of life and faith.

name: Sunday School
location: St. Paul & St. Andrew United Methodist Church
description: Weekly Sunday School for preschool and elementary children that begins in the sanctuary with an interactive message before moving to classrooms.

name: Nursery and Childcare
location: St. Paul & St. Andrew United Methodist Church
description: Professional nursery care for children ages 1-3 is available during the worship service. Check in at the front desk upon arrival for directions to the childcare room.

name: Misa en Español
description: A Spanish-language celebration of the Holy Mass held every Sunday afternoon. The service includes traditional Spanish liturgy and music, welcoming all members of the community.

name: Sunday Brunch Comedy
description: Start your Sunday afternoon with world-class comedy at the iconic Comedy Cellar on MacDougal St.

name: Freshly Squeezed
description: A ragtag gang of improvisers explores the 'absurdiverse' by looking for humor in the most unusual places. This 45-minute show features the ensemble and a special guest guest in a series of improvised explorations.

name: The Sunday Jam
description: Wind down the week with a community improv jam. Open to players of all skill levels, it's a great place to practice long-form and scene work with a friendly group.

name: Flatbush DSA Cortelyou Greenmarket Tabling
description: Engage with community members at the local greenmarket to discuss socialist initiatives and local political issues. This is a recurring outreach effort to build neighborhood power.

name: Story Time
description: A thirty-minute session celebrating books by authors and illustrators of African descent. Each session connects storytelling with mindfulness activities and themes found in the museum's permanent collection.

name: Big Apple Brunch & Day Party Each n Every Sunday

This hands-on workshop is open to all skill levels and focuses on teaching maritime craftsmanship, woodworking, and teamwork. Volunteers work together in a collaborative environment to maintain a fleet of heritage rowing vessels.

The collection measures the novelist’s growing fame at fifty-year intervals through items drawn from the collections of Sandra Clark, Mary Crawford, and Janine Barchas.

Highlights include Kerouac's childhood stories and personal letters from the collection of Jacob Loewentheil.

description: An award-winning ensemble bridging cultures through arrangements of Cuban descargas and Brazilian samba.

name: Reggae Fest Presents Alkaline
description: Reggae Fest comes to Brooklyn featuring a live performance by dancehall artist Alkaline.

name: Half-Day Tarot Intensive
description: An express masterclass covering the meanings of all 78 cards and the interpretation of full spreads. Participants will leave with a classic Rider-Waite Tarot deck and a bundle of sage to start their own practice.

name: Tristan und Isolde (Met: Live in HD)
description: Soprano Lise Davidsen stars in Wagner's ultimate dramatic role in this electrifying Met production.

name: Eddy Herrera
location: Lehman Center for the Performing Arts
description: Latin Grammy-winning merengue star Eddy Herrera returns for an exciting night of music accompanied by a full orchestra and dancers.

name: Nor'Easter Bouldering Series: Gotham Citizen
description: A bouldering competition held in Long Island City as part of the Nor'Easter Series. Participating climbers can aggregate scores for a chance to compete in the Superfinals.

name: Mai Khôi & the Dissidents: "Bad Activist"
description: Mai Khôi recounts her journey from pop stardom to activism through music, projections, and theatrical performance, followed by an album release set.

name: Bill Orcutt: “Four Louies,” Corsano/Orcutt, Ava Mendoza, & Cyrus Pireh
description: The second night of Bill Orcutt’s takeover featuring performances by Chris Corsano, Ava Mendoza, and Cyrus Pireh.
short_name: “Four Louies,” Corsano/Orcutt, Ava Mendoza, & Cyrus Pireh

name: Chair Workshop: A Flow’s Aperitivo
description: This workshop uses the chair as a steady frame to explore advanced vinyasa variations including arm balances and wheel. Led by Francesca Bove, it focuses on creating lift and freedom in familiar yoga postures.

name: Tom Kastle Presents: Joe Hill: Alive As You And Me
description: A musical play by David Simmons telling the story of legendary labor organizer and songwriter Joe Hill and the rise of the Industrial Workers of the World.
short_name: Joe Hill: Alive As You And Me

name: Emo Night Karaoke
description: A live band karaoke experience where the audience takes the stage to sing their favorite emo and pop-punk songs.

name: Currents & ERRA: The Silence Follows Tour 2026
description: A heavy night of progressive metalcore featuring leading acts Currents and ERRA. Prepare for technical riffs, powerful vocals, and a high-energy live experience.

name: Stephen Wilson Jr. - Gary The Torch Tour
description: Stephen Wilson Jr. brings his gritty, genre-blurring "Gary The Torch Tour" to the stage, blending country, rock, and soul.
short_name: Stephen Wilson Jr. - Gary The Torch Tour

name: Chris Dave × Jason Moran x Savion Glover
description: A high-concept collaboration featuring piano master Jason Moran, Savion Glover, and Chris Dave.
short_name: Chris Dave × Jason Moran x Savion Glover

name: Workshop: David McLoghlin on Sentiment
description: This workshop is dedicated to finding the right balance of sentiment so that poetry does not veer into sentimentality. Participants will work on calibration and emotional resonance in their writing.

name: Screeching Weasel, The Queers
description: A double-headline show featuring two of the most influential pop-punk bands of all time.

name: Secrets, Myths & Legends of South Street Seaport
description: Experience New York's maritime heritage in a district featuring the city's largest concentration of restored 19th-century commercial buildings. Learn about the evolution from a bustling port to a modern culinary and cultural destination.
short_name: Secrets, Myths & Legends of South Street Seaport

name: Uncovering the Historic Lower East Side
description: Uncover the history of the Lower East Side, a neighborhood that defines the classic immigrant story of New York City. Learn about the social changes and cultural heritage of the groups that built a life in this historic area.
short_name: Uncovering the Historic Lower East Side

name: The Other Side of the Gilded Age
description: Venture far from Fifth Avenue's palaces to see how everyday New Yorkers lived during the Gilded Age. Explore the streets of Chinatown and Little Italy to learn about tenement life, city workers, and the politics of the era.
short_name: The Other Side of the Gilded Age

name: Tony Vega: 50 Aniversario
description: Salsa legend Tony Vega celebrates his 50th anniversary in music with a high-energy concert. Join the celebration of a storied career filled with tropical rhythms and classic hits.

name: Euphoria / Loose Ties
description: A double-bill musical performance featuring the bands Euphoria and Loose Ties.

name: Tilted Head Ensemble: Spring 2026
description: The Tilted Head Ensemble returns to Benzaquen Hall with music by Karen Tanaka, Aníbal Vidal, Amelia Kaplan, and other contemporary composers. The performance is conducted by Carl Bettendorf.

name: PRYVT
description: Experience the atmospheric and melodic sounds of PRYVT during their live concert in Brooklyn.

name: Crossed Structure Binding
description: Construct a flexible, non-adhesive binding that allows books to open completely flat, ideal for sketchbooks.

name: Joel Veena: Ragas & Sagas with Mir Naqibul Islam
description: A concert of Hindustani slide guitar featuring traditional ragas and original electric compositions known as "sagas." Joel Veena is accompanied by tabla virtuoso Mir Naqibul Islam for a night of emotive improvisation.

name: Semyon Slepakov
description: Russian comedian and singer Semyon Slepakov performs a mix of satirical songs and stand-up comedy.

name: Spring Bazaar
description: Discover timeless treasures and unique vintage accents to refresh your style and home for the spring season.

name: Stephen Schwartz: Magic To Do
description: Broadway Playhouse introduces kids to the magical music of Stephen Schwartz, the composer behind hits like Wicked, Pippin, and Godspell.

name: Grand Pistachio Theatre: Layer the Walls Part I
description: A family-friendly theater production exploring the history of New York City through the layers of an old apartment building.

name: Desi Comedy Fest
description: A comedy showcase featuring a diverse lineup of South Asian comedians from around the world.

name: Works & Process: Martha Graham Dance Company - Jamar Roberts
description: A special preview of a new work by Jamar Roberts for the Martha Graham Dance Company's 100th season.
short_name: Martha Graham Dance Company - Jamar Roberts

name: ClimART Cafe: Mothers in Me Will Meet You There
description: A ClimART cafe discussion held in conjunction with the Nicole Cooper painting exhibition.
short_name: Mothers in Me Will Meet You There

name: Gilded Age Mansions & Memorials of Riverside Drive
location: St. Paul & St. Andrew United Methodist Church
description: Explore the Gilded Age opulence of Riverside Drive, an avenue once predicted to rival Fifth Avenue as a millionaire colony. View surviving mansions and memorials while hearing stories of the titans of industry and artists who lived along the Hudson.
short_name: Gilded Age Mansions & Memorials of Riverside Drive

name: Orkhan Zeynalli
description: Orkhan Zeynalli brings a unique fusion of traditional Azerbaijani influences and contemporary hip-hop to NYC. A must-see for fans of global rap fusion.

name: Orcutt Shelley Miller // Zeena Parkins + William Winant // Mariam Rezaei + Bill Orcutt
description: An avant-garde trio featuring members of Harry Pussy and Sonic Youth, with opening sets by two electrifying duos.
short_name: Orcutt Shelley Miller // Zeena Parkins + William Winant // Mariam Rezaei + Bill Orcutt

name: Art for the Journey: A Lenten Reflection at MoMA
description: A special contemplative guided tour at MoMA that explores religious and secular works through the lens of Lent, focusing on forgiveness, prayer, and renewal.

name: Jac Conner / Kyra Baskin / Matilde Heckler / Sophie Kyle Collins
description: Sunday night indie and folk featuring the storytelling of Jac Conner and Kyra Baskin.
short_name: Jac Conner / Kyra Baskin / Matilde Heckler / Sophie Kyle Collins

name: 30th Annual Julius Marmur Symposium
description: An annual symposium showcasing graduate student research and honoring the academic legacy of Julius Marmur.

name: The Reading Series: creation stories and all the important importants
description: This event features a reading of a new work by Mfoniso Udofia, selected for the series by curator Ayad Akhtar. It includes a staged reading and a professional conversation about the modern playwriting process.
short_name: creation stories and all the important importants

name: Little Women (1933)
location: The Sheen Center for Thought & Culture
description: Enjoy a screening of the 1933 adaptation of Little Women starring Katharine Hepburn, followed by a conversation with Dr. Matthew Clemente. This event is part of the Horizons of Hope Film Series in partnership with Boston College.

name: Jordan Ward Presents: the Apartment Tour
description: Rising R&B star Jordan Ward showcases his smooth vocals and soulful songwriting on his latest tour. His music blends classic soul influences with modern R&B vibes.
short_name: Jordan Ward Presents: the Apartment Tour

name: Vincent Dedienne en concert
description: Acclaimed French comedian Vincent Dedienne makes his US premiere in a concert based on his one-man show Un soir de gala. He performs original songs that bring new depth to his beloved stage characters.

name: Frontiers Lecture: Clouds on Worlds Beyond Our Solar System
description: Join Eileen Gonzales for a deep dive into brown dwarfs and the atmospheric molecules identified using the James Webb Space Telescope.
short_name: Clouds on Worlds Beyond Our Solar System

name: Women on the Move: Laney Katz Becker on In the Family Way
description: Award-winning author Laney Katz Becker discusses her novel about the complex lives of suburban housewives in 1965. The story explores friendships and political awakening in the era before the women's liberation movement.
short_name: Laney Katz Becker on In the Family Way

name: Big Richard
description: An all-female bluegrass/neo-acoustic supergroup performing high-energy music.

name: Artist Spotlight: Sheku & Isata Kanneh-Mason
location: David Geffen Hall at Lincoln Center
description: Siblings Sheku and Isata Kanneh-Mason perform an intimate recital of cello and piano sonatas by Mendelssohn, Schumann, and others.

name: ¡Cubanismo!
description: The acclaimed Cuban ensemble performs high-energy rumba, son, and Latin jazz.

name: Maria Somerville, Joanne Robertson
description: Irish musician Maria Somerville performs atmospheric dream pop inspired by the wild terrain of Galway's west coast. She is joined by painter and musician Joanne Robertson for an evening of improvisatory expression.

name: Steve Cavanagh: Two Kinds of Stranger
description: Steve Cavanagh presents his new title Two Kinds of Stranger at this live bookstore appearance.
short_name: Steve Cavanagh: Two Kinds of Stranger

name: Author Talk with Dr. Joanne Grasso: The American Revolution on Long Island
description: Dr. Joanne Grasso explores Long Island’s strategic and divided role during the American Revolution, including British occupation and the Culper spy ring. A Q&A and book signing follow the presentation.
short_name: The American Revolution on Long Island

name: Joyce Manor, Militarie Gun, Teen Mortgage, & Combat
description: A packed bill of punk and alternative artists headlined by Joyce Manor, known for their catchy and short-form anthems.
short_name: Joyce Manor, Militarie Gun, Teen Mortgage, & Combat

name: Workshop: Shlagha Borah: Poetics of Obsession
description: In this workshop, participants will study obsession in poetry through the lens of poetic forms. The session explores how various structures are used to meditate on a single theme or subject.

name: Obscura: Stardust USA 2026
description: Technical death metal masters Obscura bring their cosmic and complex compositions to Brooklyn.

name: Haunt, Intranced, Finix
description: A night of new wave traditional heavy metal with dual-guitar harmonies and retro energy.

name: Ester Wiesnerova
description: Composer and vocalist Ester Wiesnerova presents a captivating night of original jazz. Her performance features dynamic vocals and sophisticated musical arrangements.

name: Chuck Roth
description: New-York based guitarist and composer shares new work commissioned by Roulette.

name: The Pursuit of Liberty: How Hamilton vs. Jefferson Ignited the Lasting Battle Over Power in America
description: Jeffrey Rosen probes the opposing political views of Thomas Jefferson and Alexander Hamilton to understand the ongoing struggle for power in the U.S.
short_name: How Hamilton vs. Jefferson Ignited the Lasting Battle Over Power in America

name: LACUNA COIL: Sleepless Empire Tour 2026
description: Italian gothic metal icons Lacuna Coil headline the Sleepless Empire Tour, bringing their dual-vocal heavy sound to New York.

name: Mairead Band, Widemouth, Kitba, Sachi
description: A Tuesday night showcasing indie rock and alternative bands from the local scene.

name: Ham El-Waylly: Hello, Home Cooking
description: Chef Ham El-Waylly discusses his new cookbook, Hello, Home Cooking, sharing recipes and culinary insights.

name: Florian & Christoph Berner in Concert: An Evening of Chamber Music
description: Brothers Florian and Christoph Berner explore the profound musical dialogue between cello and piano through classical and contemporary works. The program features selections by Mozart and Beethoven alongside a contemporary piece by Austrian composer Julia Purgina.

name: Monthly Artist Meetup - March 2026
description: The March edition of the monthly networking event for artists of all disciplines looking to connect in Queens and beyond.

name: Teatro La Plaza's Hamlet
description: Written and directed by Chela De Ferrari, this production of Hamlet is performed in Spanish with projected English translation. It is presented by the award-winning Peruvian theater company Teatro La Plaza.

name: Hidden Brain: "Perceptions" Tour
description: Shankar Vedantam brings the popular Hidden Brain podcast to the stage to explore the science of human behavior.

name: Lavalle
description: Bassist Michael LaValle leads a project mixing harmonic architecture with Northeastern Brazilian rhythmic contours.

name: Matt Berninger
description: The National's frontman Matt Berninger performs a solo set featuring his signature baritone voice. Opening support for this 16+ show comes from Ronboy.

name: Rich(ard) Dawson
description: British avant-folk artist Richard Dawson performs his idiosyncratic and powerful storytelling songs.

name: “Division of Time” (World Premiere)
description: A groundbreaking celebration of music and technology featuring cellist Jeffrey Zeigler and London-based Gandini Juggling.

name: Meaghan Robichaud Is The Greatest Show on Earth
description: An anti-acrobatic solo circus performance featuring "the world's worst male clown" and fearless feats of flapdoodle.
short_name: Meaghan Robichaud Is The Greatest Show on Earth

name: Neal Medlyn: Made in Heaven
description: A performance and visual art piece featuring live music, dance, painting, and video inspired by the Jeff Koons and Cicciolina art disaster.

name: Opening Reception: Light and Whispers

name: Bird and Nature Walk: Mount Loretto Unique Area
description: Traverse the fields, woodlands, and waterfront of Mount Loretto to find wintering songbirds and early spring migrants. This guided walk offers insights into the unique ecology of the area.

name: The Kyle Dunnigan Band
description: A high-energy comedy and music performance by the Emmy-winning writer and performer Kyle Dunnigan and his band.

name: Clem Burke - Rock ‘n’ Roll Memorial & Life Celebration
description: A special memorial and celebration of life for Clem Burke, featuring live music and tributes. Musicians and fans gather to honor a significant legacy in the rock world.
short_name: Clem Burke - Rock ‘n’ Roll Memorial & Life Celebration

description: Ambassador Ido Aharoni continues his monthly update series on Israeli affairs, security, and global criticism. This session provides insider analysis of the rapidly shifting reality in the Middle East.
short_name: Israel On Our Minds – Session Two

name: Working Together in Theatre - With Joel Veenstra
description: Joel Veenstra shares insights on professional collaboration and the interpersonal dynamics of working in the theatrical industry.

name: Mae Martin: The Possum
description: Mae Martin performs their latest stand-up hour, "The Possum," blending comedy with personal observation.

name: Shabaka
description: The celebrated saxophonist and composer Shabaka Hutchings presents his latest musical explorations. The performance spans jazz, African heritage, and contemporary experimental sounds.

name: STEM Night: Women in STEM
description: A virtual event for teens and young adults highlighting the career journeys and professional advice of women working across various STEM fields.

name: Percy Jones & Mj12
description: Seminal bassist Percy Jones leads MJ12 in a set combining prog-rock, free jazz, and sly humor.

name: CupcakKe
description: Rapper CupcakKe brings her high-energy, unapologetic, and provocative performance style to the Brooklyn stage. This concert is open to fans 16 and over.

name: Spider Rabbit
description: An experimental production featuring surrealist puppetry and avant-garde performance elements.

name: Milo Rau & Édouard Louis: The Interrogation
location: NYU Skirball Center for the Performing Arts
description: A raw, intimate collaboration blending personal testimony with urgent political critique, revisiting moments of violence and class shame in France.

name: Calla
description: A raw Butoh performance exploring the tension between fragility and resilience through slow, deliberate movement.

name: Eliane Elias - The Art of Bossa Nova
description: Multi-Grammy Award winner Eliane Elias brings the smooth sounds of Brazil to NYC with her special "Art of Bossa Nova" performance.
short_name: Eliane Elias - The Art of Bossa Nova

name: Rob Osler: The Case of the Murdered Muckraker
description: Rob Osler visits the shop to discuss The Case of the Murdered Muckraker.
short_name: The Case of the Murdered Muckraker

name: Zara Larsson
description: Global pop star Zara Larsson brings her chart-topping hits and impressive choreography to the Brooklyn stage.

name: Unearthing the Earth: Architectural Histories of Extractivism
description: This HAUS-hosted symposium explores how to historicize extractivism's long durée from the perspective of architectural history.

name: Mike Gordon
description: Phish bassist Mike Gordon leads his solo project through an evening of innovative rock and unique grooves.

name: Rain Garden Stewardship Volunteer Days
