name: The Technicolors
description: Alternative rock group The Technicolors perform a live set featuring their blend of psychedelic and indie influences.

name: The Dollop - The Dollop Podcast Live
description: Comedians Dave Anthony and Gareth Reynolds record a live episode of their hit history podcast, exploring a weird and wild story from American history.
short_name: The Dollop - The Dollop Podcast Live

name: oskar med k: Make Me Feel Tour 2026
description: Oskar med k brings his signature electronic and house-inspired sound to a late-night slot as part of his 2026 world tour. The event offers a modern club atmosphere with driving dance beats.
short_name: oskar med k: Make Me Feel Tour 2026

name: Gogol Bordello
description: The legendary gypsy punk band brings their chaotic and infectious energy for a high-octane live show. Expect a riotous blend of Eastern European folk, punk rock, and theatrical performance.

name: Enigma Variations & Bruch’s Violin Concerto
location: David Geffen Hall at Lincoln Center
description: Nikolaj Szeps-Znaider leads Bruch’s Violin Concerto No. 1 and Elgar’s legendary Enigma Variations.

name: Kyle Kinane
description: Kyle Kinane brings his gravelly voice and unique observations to the stage for a night of stand-up.

name: Ernest: Live from the South Tour
description: Country singer-songwriter ERNEST brings his "LIVE FROM THE SOUTH TOUR" to the Big Apple. Known for his storytelling, he delivers an authentic and heartfelt country music experience.
short_name: Ernest: Live from the South Tour

name: Robot Song
description: A dazzling theater work for kids and adults celebrating inclusion and creativity.

name: Boojum & Hot Like Mars
description: Loop Troupe presents "Electric Wonderland on Mars," a cosmic and funky live music experience.

name: Aaron Burnett and the Big Machine: Quantum Breaks II
description: New “Jazztronica” compositions inspired by electronic music techniques, UK drum ‘n’ bass, and hip-hop.

name: Steven Wendt: Plato Caves: Screens vs Shadows
description: Scientist Steven explores how shadows and sounds can match up to make feelings happen, loosely using Plato’s Allegory of the Cave. This performance is open to all ages, specifically designed for children 5 to 12 years old.

name: Bill Frisell: 75th Birthday Celebration
description: "In My Dreams" features Frisell with longtime collaborators Jenny Scheinman, Hank Roberts, and others in a sonic reunion.

name: Freak & Donna & Friends: The Finale!
description: This grand finale show features the premiere of the Freak & Donna series ender along with live talent performances.

name: Bach: St. John Passion
description: A deeply moving musical journey through Christ’s final hours, featuring intimate arias, urgent choruses, and beautiful chorales. This Lent concert offers a profound musical gateway into the heart of Holy Week.

name: Fagtasia Presents: She-Cago
description: A high-energy, campy, and theatrical comedy show brought to life by the Fagtasia performers.

name: Solar Cross, Invunche, Conifere, Iron Firmament, Keys to the Astral Gates, Druhtiz
description: A dark assembly of black metal and atmospheric extreme music featuring diverse international acts.
short_name: Solar Cross, Invunche, Conifere, Iron Firmament, Keys to the Astral Gates, Druhtiz

name: Teacher, Teacher: Kiyoshi Kurosawa’s Serpent’s Path and Chime
description: A double feature highlighting the works of Kiyoshi Kurosawa, including the gangland thriller Serpent’s Path and the psychological featurette Chime.
short_name: Kiyoshi Kurosawa’s Serpent’s Path and Chime

name: Neuroscience WIP series: Emily Schoenblum, Roozie Coenen
description: Research presentations from Emily Schoenblum and Roozie Coenen as part of the neuroscience works in progress series.

name: An Evening With Lynne Koplitz. Special Event.
description: Renowned comedian Lynne Koplitz performs a special headline set at the Broadway Comedy Club.

name: Alpha
description: A troubled 13-year-old and her mother face a collapsing world after she returns from school with a mysterious new tattoo.

name: Will Burkart
description: Comedian Will Burkart performs two sets of his viral observational stand-up comedy and crowd work.

name: They Will Kill You
description: A housekeeper in a New York City high-rise uncovers links to a possible satanic cult in this thriller directed by Kirill Sokolov.

name: Etienne De Crecy, Justin Strauss
description: French Touch architect Etienne De Crecy joins forces with New York club legend Justin Strauss for a night of timeless house music.

name: Kareem Green: Cut The Malarkey- Live at Comedy In Harlem
description: Headliner Kareem Green brings his sharp wit and real-life storytelling to two must-see shows featuring Shatara Curry.

name: Féile na Gaeilge / Irish Language Day 2026
description: A day-long celebration of the Irish language featuring story-focused workshops, conversation circles, and short films.
short_name: Féile na Gaeilge / Irish Language Day 2026

name: Gearrscannáin na Gaeilge / Irish Language Shorts
description: A screening of contemporary Irish language short films across various genres including comedy, drama, and history.
short_name: Gearrscannáin na Gaeilge / Irish Language Shorts

name: Seanchoíche
description: A storytelling night where volunteer speakers share personal narratives and poetry in the Irish language on the theme of "Home."

name: The Music of Taylor Swift: A Symphonic Tribute
description: A full orchestra performs symphonic arrangements of Taylor Swift's greatest hits from throughout her career.

name: Old 97's
description: Alt-country pioneers Old 97's return to the stage with support from artist Lizzie No. The band is celebrated for their energetic blend of power pop and traditional country roots.

name: Cock Sparrer
description: Legendary Oi! and punk rock band Cock Sparrer celebrate decades of anthemic songs for a two-night stand.

name: Mister Cobra
description: Lucy Liyou combines Korean folk opera and drag-inspired techniques in a solo performance confronting the boundaries of transition and personhood.

name: Uncle Vanya, scenes from country life
description: A new production of Chekhov’s classic play depicting the complexities of human longing and regret.
short_name: Uncle Vanya, scenes from country life

name: Kyle Kinane
description: Stand-up comedian Kyle Kinane brings his unique observations and storytelling to the stage for a night of laughs.

name: Hekate Supper: Performative Lecture with Artist Kay Turner
description: Artist and scholar Kay Turner presents a performative lecture on the archetype of the Greek goddess Hekate. The session explores mythology related to crossroads and the moon, including historical ritual suppers.

name: Street Tree Care with plant xchange
description: Partner with plant xchange for a volunteer street tree care session. Help steward the urban canopy through mulching, weeding, and general maintenance of city trees.

name: Philharmonic Families: Winds
description: An interactive concert designed for children that focuses on the wind family of the orchestra. Musicians from the Philharmonic demonstrate the unique sounds of the woodwinds in a fun environment.

name: Wynonna In Concert
description: Country music superstar Wynonna Judd performs a Red Carpet GALA benefit concert. The evening celebrates the best of country music while supporting the mission of the non-profit St. George Theatre.

name: Little Syria & the Lower West Side
description: Remember the vital immigrant community of the Lower West Side and the Arabic-speaking enclave known as Little Syria. This tour uncovers the history of a neighborhood that served as the mother colony for the Arab American diaspora.
short_name: Little Syria & the Lower West Side

name: Lavoe En Sinfónica
location: Lehman Center for the Performing Arts
description: Award-winning actor and vocalist Raúl Carbonell reprises his legendary portrayal of Héctor Lavoe in this special theatrical concert. Lavoe’s timeless anthems are elevated by the Héctor Lavoe Band and a full symphonic ensemble.

name: ripple & rip: marbling & collage
description: A contemporary take on the traditional art of marbling combined with collage techniques. Instructor Nazli Efe leads this two-session exploration of mixed media paper arts.

name: Laveda
description: Ethereal shoegaze and dreampop project Laveda performs live, bringing lush soundscapes to Night Club 101.

name: Tennis Courts, Paper Lady, Range Life
description: A late-night triple bill featuring the guitar-driven indie and dream-pop sounds of Tennis Courts, Paper Lady, and Range Life.
short_name: Tennis Courts, Paper Lady, Range Life

name: Last Heroes: OPIA Tour
description: Electronic duo Last Heroes brings their melodic bass and cinematic soundscapes to NYC for the OPIA Tour.

name: Total Mass Retain - YES Tribute Band
description: A tribute band performing the intricate and symphonic progressive rock music of YES.
short_name: Total Mass Retain - YES Tribute Band

name: Emergency Tiara, Evil Jake, frank, This is A Low
description: A powerhouse Saturday night lineup featuring the pop-soul style of Emergency Tiara and rock from Evil Jake.
short_name: Emergency Tiara, Evil Jake, frank, This is A Low

name: Nidia Góngora: Afro-Colombian Music
description: Experience the soulful currents of the Colombian Pacific coast with Latin Grammy-nominated singer Nidia Góngora.

name: Israeli Chamber Project
description: An ensemble of award-winning Israeli musicians performs a program of classical and contemporary chamber works.

name: Drain
description: California hardcore band Drain brings their high-intensity, surf-influenced thrash to the stage. Be prepared for a high-energy show and significant crowd participation.

name: Open Movement: Subject to Object: Self as Text
description: Jerron Harmon leads a workshop centering disability aesthetics to translate personal memory into shared movement and sound contexts.
short_name: Subject to Object: Self as Text

name: Meshell Ndegeocello
description: As part of NYC Winter Jazzfest, this performance features the restlessly collaborative and multi-disciplinary artistry of Meshell Ndegeocello.

name: Brooklyn Nets vs. Sacramento Kings
description: The Brooklyn Nets take on the Sacramento Kings in a Sunday matchup. Close out the weekend with world-class NBA action in Brooklyn.

name: Milo Rau & Servane Dècle: The Pelicot Trial
description: An urgent North American premiere featuring readings of examinations, pleas, and commentaries from the historic Mazan rape case trial. This production confronts the global system of patriarchy and violence against women.

name: Artists, Oddballs & Provocateurs: East Village Tour
description: Explore the East Village's legacy as a haven for artists, oddballs, and provocateurs. Learn about the neighborhood's role in counterculture and the creative figures who have left a lasting impression on its streets.

name: Virtual Stations of the Cross
description: An online time of prayer and reflection exploring 15 stations of the cross through visual art, music, scripture, and prayer.

name: Organ Recital: Ken Cowan
description: Master organist Ken Cowan demonstrates the power and versatility of St. Bart's organ in a program of virtuosic compositions. One of the world's largest pipe organs is showcased in this solo recital.

name: Beethoven’s Fifth Symphony
description: Host Rob Kapilow breaks down the intricacies and genius of Beethoven’s iconic Fifth Symphony in a lecture-performance format.

name: Works & Process: The Metropolitan Opera - Kaija Saariaho’s Innocence
description: A preview of the final opera by Kaija Saariaho with excerpts and a discussion moderated by Peter Gelb.
short_name: The Metropolitan Opera - Kaija Saariaho’s Innocence

name: Drain
description: Hardcore powerhouse Drain brings their aggressive energy and thrash-influenced sound to the stage. Prepare for an intense night of heavy riffs and high-octane performance.

name: African Apocalypse Movie Screening
description: A screening of African Apocalypse followed by a Q&A with filmmaker Rob Lemkin. The film follows the trail of colonial soldier Paul Voulet and explores the legacy of destruction in Nigerian communities.

name: Die Krupps 45th Anniversary
description: Industrial metal and EBM pioneers Die Krupps celebrate 45 years of their influential sound with a special career-spanning anniversary concert.

name: Nmixx 1st World Tour [Episode 1: Zero Frontier]
description: K-pop sensation NMIXX brings their "MIXX POP" sound and powerful choreography for their first world tour.

name: Eilen Jewell
description: Singer-songwriter Eilen Jewell performs her signature blend of country, noir-blues, and soulful roots music.

name: Brooklyn Nets vs. Charlotte Hornets
description: The Brooklyn Nets host the Charlotte Hornets in a Tuesday night conference game. Catch all the dunks and three-pointers live in Brooklyn.

name: Screening | Pull My Daisy, Musical Performance, Q&A
description: A special event featuring the screening of the Beat classic 'Pull My Daisy' alongside a live musical performance and discussion.
short_name: Screening | Pull My Daisy, Musical Performance, Q&A

name: Sally Baby's Silver Dollars
description: A musical performance featuring the soulful vocal styling and classic arrangements of Sally Baby's Silver Dollars.

name: Moonchild: Waves Tour
description: The LA-based trio Moonchild brings their smooth blend of alternative R&B and jazz to NYC. Their lush arrangements and soulful harmonies create a beautiful and relaxed live atmosphere.

name: Film Screening: Woman at War
description: The environmental fable "Woman at War" follows an Icelandic activist waging a solo war on the local aluminum industry.

name: Madison Cunningham: The Ace Tour
description: Grammy-winning singer-songwriter and guitarist Madison Cunningham performs music from her acclaimed albums as part of her Ace Tour.

name: Stick Men
description: Progressive rock trio featuring members of King Crimson performing on the Chapman Stick and electronic percussion.

name: Book Launch: Susan Cheever
description: Author Susan Cheever discusses her new book When All the Men Wore Hats with Chair of Fiction Luis Jaramillo. The book explores mid-century culture through reflections on her father's work.

name: Ryan Caraveo: Forever Foggy Tour
description: Ryan Caraveo brings his vulnerable and genre-blending hip-hop to the stage as part of his national tour.

name: rusowsky
description: Spanish artist rusowsky brings his unique blend of bedroom pop and electronic music to Brooklyn. His sound often incorporates jazz and R&B elements into a modern indie framework.

name: Season Finale: Jeffrey Siegel
description: Pianist Jeffrey Siegel closes the Keyboard Conversations season with uplifting music by Bach, Grieg, and Schumann.

name: The Meteors
description: The kings of psychobilly return to deliver their unique brand of high-octane rockabilly-punk.

name: Rachel Sumner & Traveling Light
description: The Boston-based trio performs 'Femericana'—a blend of folk tradition with feminist storytelling and poetic detail. Led by award-winning songwriter Rachel Sumner, the group features spare, intimate arrangements for guitar, bass, and fiddle.

name: David Cross: Shootin’ the Shit, Seein’ What Sticks
description: Legendary comedian David Cross performs his show "Shootin’ the Shit, Seein’ What Sticks" in an intimate setting at Union Hall.
short_name: Shootin’ the Shit, Seein’ What Sticks

name: Hunter Hayes: The Evergreen Tour
description: Multi-instrumentalist and country star Hunter Hayes brings his Evergreen Tour to NYC for an evening of chart-topping hits and new material.

name: Pearly Drops
description: Finnish synth-pop duo Pearly Drops performs their ethereal and dreamy soundscapes with support from Teather.

name: Shimza
description: South African DJ Shimza brings his powerful Afro-tech and deep house grooves to the main stage.

name: On Cinema – the Certified “Five Bags of Popcorn” Tour
description: Tim Heidecker and Gregg Turkington bring their legendary movie review show to life on the Five Bags of Popcorn Tour. A must-see for fans of the cult-favorite On Cinema universe.
short_name: On Cinema – the Certified “Five Bags of Popcorn” Tour

name: The Brook & The Bluff Werewolf Tour
description: Nashville-based band The Brook & The Bluff brings their Werewolf Tour and signature harmonies to Webster Hall.
short_name: The Brook & The Bluff Werewolf Tour

name: Alash Ensemble
description: Masters of Tuvan throat singing, the Alash trio performs traditional music infused with subtle modern influences. They showcase the remarkable technique of singing multiple pitches simultaneously while incorporating western musical elements.

name: Nothing
description: Shoegaze band Nothing brings their atmospheric and heavy wall-of-sound to Brooklyn. Known for their immersive live shows, the band blends melody with crushing volume.

name: Brooklyn Nets vs. Atlanta Hawks
description: The Atlanta Hawks take on the Brooklyn Nets in an April NBA matchup. Enjoy professional basketball as the regular season heats up at Barclays Center.

name: Off the Record: Acts of Restorative Justice
description: A participatory theatrical intervention aimed at the American Criminal Justice System that offers free consultations with criminal justice attorneys. The work is a collaboration between James Scruggs and Thomas Giovanni designed to offer real aid to those with criminal records.

name: Level Up & Zingara
description: A heavy-hitting night of bass and dubstep featuring a back-to-back performance by two rising stars.

name: Thievery Corporation
description: The acclaimed electronic duo Thievery Corporation brings their eclectic mix of acid jazz, downtempo, and world music to the venue. The show features a full live band performance for ages 16 and over.

name: Lontalius and gabby start
description: New Zealand singer-songwriter Lontalius performs bedroom pop with gabby start.

name: Scorched Earth
description: A new production appearing at St. Ann's Warehouse for a limited run in April. This performance is a featured part of the current 2025-26 season lineup.

name: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie
description: The Super Mario Bros. return in this animated galactic adventure produced by Illumination and Nintendo. Following the 2023 global hit, this sequel brings the iconic world of Mario Galaxy to the big screen.

name: The Dirty Three
description: The legendary Australian instrumental rock trio performs in New York City for the first time since 2009. Known for their unhinged and savage live shows, the band mixes punk gusto with improvisational jazz elements.

name: Ivy Baldwin Dance + Jeanine Durning
location: NYU Skirball Center for the Performing Arts
description: A world premiere double bill featuring bold new works from two of contemporary dance's most fearless voices. This evening pairs Baldwin’s rigorously inventive physical landscapes with Durning’s radical experiments in movement and language.

name: Witchz Presents Enter Afterlife World Tour 2026
description: The mysterious WITCHZ brings the Enter Afterlife World Tour to New York, featuring a fusion of dark pop, trap, and electronic soundscapes.
short_name: Witchz Presents Enter Afterlife World Tour 2026

name: Ranger Trucco
description: One of tech house's fastest rising names, Ranger Trucco, performs a set of heavy grooves and original vocals.

name: Daniel Donato: Cosmic Country
description: Daniel Donato brings his 'Cosmic Country' sound to the Beacon Theatre, merging traditional honky-tonk with the improvisational spirit of jam bands. It's a night of virtuosic guitar work and modern Americana.

name: 49th & Main
description: Irish indie-pop duo 49th & Main performs a headline set featuring their blend of house-influenced pop music.

name: Angel Du$t
description: Hardcore supergroup Angel Du$t performs live with support from Outta Pocket, Jivebomb, and Vanity for a high-intensity evening of punk.

name: Bywater Call
description: A powerful 7-piece band blending southern soul, gospel, and blues-rock into a high-energy live experience.

name: morning dew: cyanotype & natural toners
description: Explore alternative photographic printing using organic materials like green tea, coffee, and wine. Instructor Mickey Aloisio leads sessions on cameraless photograms and contact printing from digital negatives.

name: 1tbsp
description: The electronic project of Maxwell Byrne delivers a night of indie-dance and house rhythms with support from Soltera.

name: Lpr Presents: Baths 15th Anniversary of Cerulean
description: Celebrate the 15th anniversary of the album Cerulean with a live performance by Baths. This special anniversary tour stop is presented by LPR at littlefield.

name: Queens Night Market
description: The Queens Night Market is a large, family-friendly open-air market featuring over 100 independent vendors selling international food, merchandise, and art. The event celebrates the rich cultural diversity of NYC with affordable prices and small-scale cultural performances.

name: WeBop Family Jazz Party: Jazz & Africa, Diaspora Connections
description: An interactive, high-energy musical adventure for families with children aged 8 months to 8 years. The WeBop Family Jazz Band invites kids to dance, sing, and explore the joyful rhythms of Africa and the diaspora.

name: Tigers Jaw with Hot Flash Heat Wave
description: Indie rock stalwarts Tigers Jaw perform with special guests Hot Flash Heat Wave for an evening of emo-tinged alternative rock.

name: Kenny K'ore
description: A live performance featuring the vocal and musical artistry of Kenny K'ore.

name: James McMurtry
description: Acclaimed singer-songwriter James McMurtry performs his blend of folk-rock and Americana alongside BettySoo. Known for his sharp lyrical narratives, this is an essential show for folk music fans.

name: Moby-Dick: Reading the White Whale
description: A seminar-style reading group focused on Herman Melville's classic maritime masterpiece, Moby-Dick.

name: Klezmer and Other Displaced Musics in America
description: Scholar Walter Zev Feldman explores the background of the klezmer revitalization in 1960s American cities. Drawing from his memoir, he reflects on learning from Greek and eminent klezmer musicians.
short_name: Klezmer and Other Displaced Musics in America

name: Mannes Sounds: Pianists at the Opera
description: A donor appreciation concert exploring 19th-century grand opera through the lens of virtuoso piano techniques. Curated by Donald Wagner, the concert highlights the piano's ability to capture operatic grandeur.

name: Women on the Move: Judy Batalion on The Last Woman of Warsaw
description: National Jewish Book Award winner Judy Batalion discusses her debut novel about two Jewish women in Warsaw facing the danger of war. She explores the connection between historical research on resistance fighters and the process of writing fiction.
short_name: Judy Batalion on The Last Woman of Warsaw

name: Wasted x Van Alen
description: Pratt Institute visits Van Alen for their annual public conversation exploring the status of waste in architecture and the potential for reuse with minimal energy and capital expenditure.

name: The Albertine Book Club Reads "Free Day" by Inès Cagnati
description: A book club discussion of Inès Cagnati's novel Free Day, a translated French classic published by The New York Review of Books.
short_name: The Albertine Book Club Reads "Free Day" by Inès Cagnati

name: Golda Schultz Sings Barber & Stravinsky
location: David Geffen Hall at Lincoln Center
description: Soprano Golda Schultz joins conductor Kwamé Ryan for Samuel Barber’s Knoxville: Summer of 1915. The program also features the world premiere of a new work by George Lewis.

name: Bell X1 & Dowry Strings
description: Multi-platinum-selling indie rock trio Bell X1 performs new interpretations of their 25-year catalog. They are joined by the cross-genre ensemble Dowry Strings for this anticipated return to New York.

name: Graham100: The Centennial Celebration
description: The Martha Graham Dance Company celebrates its 100th anniversary with a special centennial program. The event honors the legacy and innovation of one of the most influential figures in modern dance history.

name: Monthly Jazz Jam - April 2026 - Honoring Billie Holiday
description: Join the Flushing Town Hall jazz community for a tribute to the legendary Billie Holiday. Musicians of all backgrounds are welcome to perform and improvise together during this monthly jam session.

name: New Directors/New Films 2026
description: Jointly presented with MoMA, this prestigious festival showcases emerging directors at the vanguard of cinema, continuing its long history of discovering major global talent.

name: kwn
description: An intimate performance showcasing the soulful R&B sounds of emerging artist kwn.

name: Bird and Nature Walk: Freshkills-North Park
description: Witness the return of spring migrants in the unique landscape of Freshkills Park. The walk focuses on identifying raptors and migrating waterfowl within this significant environmental restoration project.

name: Jarlath Regan: Gas Man
description: Acclaimed Irish comedian and podcast host Jarlath Regan performs two shows of his latest stand-up special, 'Gas Man'.

name: My Excellency — With Jonathan Cohen & Gy Mirano
description: An intimate evening featuring Jonathan Cohen and Gy Mirano as they discuss their latest artistic project. The event explores the intersection of performance and literature.

name: Jim Gaffigan: Everything is Wonderful! Tour
description: Seven-time Grammy-nominated comedian Jim Gaffigan stops at the Beacon Theatre with his new world tour. Known for his relatable observational humor about family and food, Gaffigan remains one of the world's top stand-up performers.

name: kwn tour 2026
description: Emerging artist kwn visits Webster Hall as part of their 2026 tour, delivering a fresh and unique pop sound.

name: Bluegrass Improvisation for All Instruments with Stash Wyslouch - A Workshop
description: Iconoclastic flatpicker Stash Wyslouch leads a workshop on melodic and rhythmic development for bluegrass improvisation. Open to all instruments, the class covers practice routines and foundational skills.

name: Mattie & Debbie Album Release Party
description: Sean Trischka and Stash Wyslouch celebrate their debut duo album 'Satan’s Junction'. The performance features tight vocal harmonies, virtuosic bluegrass picking, and a mix of humorous and heartfelt anthems.

name: brooklyn fine art print fair
description: The fair's second edition convenes galleries, print publishers, and academic departments for a celebration of contemporary printmaking. This four-day event features leading independent artists.

name: The Belair Lip Bombs, Dust
description: Australian indie-rock band The Belair Lip Bombs performs live with support from the group Dust.

name: Mannes Sounds: Romance is in the Air
description: A multi-genre donor appreciation concert featuring works by Brahms, Bernstein, Puccini, and Cole Porter. The event showcases students from Mannes, the School of Jazz, and the School of Drama.
short_name: Mannes Sounds: Romance is in the Air

name: The Festival in Da Back
description: A richly textured and fiercely funny play about family legacy and the music that moves us, set in the crawfish capital of the world. Ghosts hum through the zydeco as the story explores who we crown, what we bury, and whether the music can still bring us home.

name: NateWantsToBattle: Phantom Burial Tour
description: NateWantsToBattle headlines a night of anime-inspired rock and pop-punk with guests Cam Steady and GENWUNNER.

name: Luke Waldron
description: An evening performance by singer-songwriter Luke Waldron in an intimate venue setting.

name: Rachel Khong + Mary H.K. Choi: My Dear You
description: Award-winning writer Rachel Khong discusses her new short story collection, My Dear You, with Mary H.K. Choi.

name: Natalia Lafourcade
description: Mexican singer-songwriter Natalia Lafourcade gets back to basics with an intimate new tour performing hits and new tracks.

name: Steal This Story, Please!
description: A provocative look behind the scenes of journalist Amy Goodman's reports on the most consequential and routinely silenced stories of our time.

name: Steph Tolev: Keepin' 'Em Hard
description: Canadian comedian Steph Tolev brings her high-energy, raw, and physical stand-up to the stage for a night of unapologetic humor.

name: Two Feet: The Next Steps Tour
description: Two Feet performs his atmospheric blend of bluesy guitar riffs and heavy electronic beats live.
short_name: Two Feet: The Next Steps Tour

name: Mo Gilligan - The Mo You Know
description: Triple BAFTA-winning British comedian Mo Gilligan brings his massive world tour 'The Mo You Know' to the Gramercy Theatre.
short_name: Mo Gilligan - The Mo You Know

name: Open Set Song Contest Performance
description: An 'open mic' style evening where winners of the math song competition perform original lyrics inspired by mathematics. It showcases the creative side of math through various musical genres.

name: Mike Massé: Epic Acoustic Classic Rock
description: Mike Massé performs his celebrated acoustic covers of legendary classic rock anthems.
short_name: Mike Massé: Epic Acoustic Classic Rock

name: Subhumans and La Pobreska at TV EYE
description: Legendary UK punk band Subhumans takes the stage at TV EYE with support from La Pobreska. This high-energy show features a night of classic punk and ska-influenced rock in an intimate venue setting.
